parityManifest.title: Purpose-built small web-task coding loop fixture

capabilities.name: inspect-repository-context
capabilities.description: The agent can read the fixture repo, inspect the existing code paths, and identify the intended change surface before editing.
observableCompletionCriteria: Reads the fixture app files that define the task surface before making edits.
Identifies the target route or component that must change for the fixture task.
Uses tracked fixture files instead of relying on ephemeral runtime artifacts.
capabilities.currentGap: Repo-mode and installed-mode proofs cover this capability, but remaining parity gaps stay in smoke-runner, fixtures-runner, live-runner, live-regression-runner, e2e-smoke, e2e-headless.

capabilities.name: edit-application-code
capabilities.description: The agent can modify the fixture application code to satisfy the requested web-task behavior.
observableCompletionCriteria: Applies a code change to the fixture app rather than only editing test snapshots or metadata.
Leaves the fixture in a runnable state after the change.
Produces a diff that can be checked in git-tracked fixture files.
capabilities.currentGap: Repo-mode and installed-mode proofs cover this capability, but remaining parity gaps stay in smoke-runner, fixtures-runner, live-runner, live-regression-runner, e2e-smoke, e2e-headless.

capabilities.name: run-targeted-tests
capabilities.description: The agent can execute the fixture's targeted tests and use the result as part of the coding loop.
observableCompletionCriteria: Runs the fixture's task-scoped test command after editing.
Reports pass/fail status with exit code visibility.
Uses the test result to determine whether the fixture task is complete.
capabilities.currentGap: Repo-mode and installed-mode proofs cover this capability, but remaining parity gaps stay in smoke-runner, fixtures-runner, live-runner, live-regression-runner, e2e-smoke, e2e-headless.

capabilities.name: manage-dev-server-lifecycle
capabilities.description: The agent can start, observe, and stop the fixture application's dev server as part of the proof path.
observableCompletionCriteria: Starts the fixture dev server without hanging the parent process.
Waits for readiness with an observable signal rather than arbitrary sleeps.
Stops or cleans up the dev server after verification.
capabilities.currentGap: Repo-mode and installed-mode proofs cover this capability, but remaining parity gaps stay in smoke-runner, fixtures-runner, live-runner, live-regression-runner, e2e-smoke, e2e-headless.

capabilities.name: verify-browser-behavior
capabilities.description: The agent can use browser verification to confirm the fixture's user-visible web behavior after the code change.
observableCompletionCriteria: Navigates to the running fixture app in a browser context.
Checks the intended UI state with explicit assertions rather than prose only.
Captures enough observable evidence to diagnose a parity failure.
capabilities.currentGap: Repo-mode and installed-mode proofs cover this capability, but remaining parity gaps stay in smoke-runner, fixtures-runner, live-runner, live-regression-runner, e2e-smoke, e2e-headless.

uncoveredCapabilities.currentGap: Repo-mode and installed-mode proofs cover this capability, but remaining parity gaps stay in smoke-runner, fixtures-runner, live-runner, live-regression-runner, e2e-smoke, e2e-headless.

uncoveredAreas.summary: No dedicated secondary-surface parity artifact reports web-mode coverage in the parity release workflow.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Publish a machine-readable lane/report entry so release-gate consumers can read web-mode parity without re-inferring it from individual tests.

uncoveredAreas.summary: Current web-mode evidence is integration-heavy but not framed as installed-mode parity proof alongside repo-mode parity.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Define whether web mode needs repo/install dual proof or an explicit scoped exception in the parity matrix.

surfaces.title: MCP server and client parity surface

uncoveredAreas.summary: No secondary parity lane currently emits MCP-specific covered/uncovered status into a tracked parity artifact.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Add a deterministic MCP parity contract/report lane so downstream slices stop treating MCP support as an implicit side effect of unit tests.

uncoveredAreas.summary: Existing MCP tests do not yet demonstrate the installed packaged CLI serving a representative MCP parity interaction in the parity report surface.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Define a representative MCP interaction and attach it to the parity matrix.

uncoveredAreas.summary: No tracked parity fixture yet exercises one representative workflow/BMAD path as a release-readable contract.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Define a deterministic workflow fixture or manifest entry so the parity report can state exactly what workflow parity means.

uncoveredAreas.summary: BMAD-relevant workflow coverage exists only indirectly through command/action contracts and shipped workflow assets.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Publish explicit scope semantics describing which workflow/BMAD experiences are in or out of parity scope.

surfaces.title: Worktree, session, and recovery parity surface

uncoveredAreas.summary: Worktree/session/recovery evidence is scattered across CLI and web tests but not summarized in a parity artifact.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Publish one machine-readable matrix row that downstream slices and release gates can consume directly.

uncoveredAreas.summary: There is no deterministic installed-mode parity fixture demonstrating representative worktree/session recovery behavior beyond individual contract tests.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Decide whether a worktree/session fixture is required or whether scoped contract coverage is sufficient for parity closure.

uncoveredAreas.summary: No dedicated secondary-surface parity artifact reports web-mode coverage in the parity release workflow.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Publish a machine-readable lane/report entry so release-gate consumers can read web-mode parity without re-inferring it from individual tests.

uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Define whether web mode needs repo/install dual proof or an explicit scoped exception in the parity matrix.

driftFindings.match: [gsd] Warning: RTK unavailable — continuing without shell-command compression
driftFindings.whyItMatters: Runtime stderr still brands the shipped CLI as gsd, so parity and support logs misidentify umb sessions.
Non-interactive guidance still tells users to run gsd subcommands, which is visible in real failure output.
The renamed CLI still advertises web mode under the old command name in TTY failure guidance.
Worktree subcommand errors still expose the old executable name in user-visible usage text.
Worktree removal guidance still references gsd, creating rename drift on a key secondary surface.
MCP hosts and logs still see the old brand during server startup.
Web-mode operational diagnostics still emit the old prefix in normal runtime flows.
Packaging metadata still publishes the old image naming convention instead of the renamed product surface.
Internal comments still encode the old package root, which can mislead future maintenance around packaged web mode.
The live-regression lane still documents global install using the old package name, weakening parity-report trust.
Tests still codify the old install package name, so downstream packaging work must retire this drift intentionally.
Packaged web-mode tests still model the old global package root, so parity claims need an explicit drift note rather than silent acceptance.

surfaces.title: Browser-only web mode parity surface
surfaces.scopeBoundary: Covers the shipped `umb --web` / `umb web` launch path, host/network flags, onboarding lock state, command-surface parity, and recovery/session diagnostics exposed in the web UI. It does not yet prove a dedicated parity fixture through the release-gate artifact.

alreadyCoveredBy.note: Verifies CLI branching, path handling, launch/stop behavior, and package script expectations for web mode.
Exercises browser onboarding lock/unlock behavior for fresh web sessions.
Locks command-surface session, resume, rename, and settings state shared by the web UI.
Locks recovery diagnostics and git-summary state on the shared web command surface.

uncoveredAreas.summary: No dedicated secondary-surface parity artifact reports web-mode coverage in the parity release workflow.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Publish a machine-readable lane/report entry so release-gate consumers can read web-mode parity without re-inferring it from individual tests.

uncoveredAreas.summary: Current web-mode evidence is integration-heavy but not framed as installed-mode parity proof alongside repo-mode parity.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Define whether web mode needs repo/install dual proof or an explicit scoped exception in the parity matrix.

surfaces.title: MCP server and client parity surface
surfaces.scopeBoundary: Covers `--mode mcp` startup, packaged MCP module importability, bundled MCP extension conflicts, and MCP-related package server contracts. It does not yet prove an end-to-end secondary parity lane that a downstream release gate can read as MCP parity.

alreadyCoveredBy.note: Shipped native MCP server entrypoint exists and is wired from the CLI mode switch.
Verifies MCP server module import/start behavior at the built artifact layer.
Locks the MCP client tool schema contract.
Verifies packaged MCP server tool exposure for ask_user_questions.

uncoveredAreas.summary: No secondary parity lane currently emits MCP-specific covered/uncovered status into a tracked parity artifact.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Add a deterministic MCP parity contract/report lane so downstream slices stop treating MCP support as an implicit side effect of unit tests.

uncoveredAreas.summary: Existing MCP tests do not yet demonstrate the installed packaged CLI serving a representative MCP parity interaction in the parity report surface.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Define a representative MCP interaction and attach it to the parity matrix.

surfaces.title: Workflow / BMAD representative parity surface
surfaces.scopeBoundary: Covers representative workflow command routing, workflow CTA execution, and the shipped skill/workflow assets that shape guided flows. It does not yet include a deterministic parity fixture that proves a representative workflow/BMAD loop end to end.

alreadyCoveredBy.note: Locks workflow control derivation and exposed action contracts.
Verifies workflow action execution plumbing in the web layer.
Verifies command-root routing, including `/gsd status` surface dispatch and passthrough behavior.
Tracked workflow assets exist, proving the product ships workflow-oriented guidance surfaces.

uncoveredAreas.summary: No tracked parity fixture yet exercises one representative workflow/BMAD path as a release-readable contract.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Define a deterministic workflow fixture or manifest entry so the parity report can state exactly what workflow parity means.

uncoveredAreas.summary: BMAD-relevant workflow coverage exists only indirectly through command/action contracts and shipped workflow assets.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Publish explicit scope semantics describing which workflow/BMAD experiences are in or out of parity scope.

surfaces.title: Worktree, session, and recovery parity surface
surfaces.scopeBoundary: Covers CLI session listing/resume, headless resume semantics, worktree subcommands/help, and web command-surface recovery/session diagnostics. It does not yet publish a dedicated parity artifact that unifies those proofs and the remaining drift points.

alreadyCoveredBy.note: Defines the user-facing worktree and sessions help contract for the renamed CLI.
Implements resume-session parsing and error reporting for headless mode.
Verifies `--resume` failure messaging and headless output behavior.
Locks recovery diagnostics state and payload contracts for the web surface.
Locks session-browser, resume, rename, and settings state on the web command surface.

uncoveredAreas.summary: Worktree/session/recovery evidence is scattered across CLI and web tests but not summarized in a parity artifact.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Publish one machine-readable matrix row that downstream slices and release gates can consume directly.

uncoveredAreas.summary: There is no deterministic installed-mode parity fixture demonstrating representative worktree/session recovery behavior beyond individual contract tests.
uncoveredAreas.downstreamNeed: Decide whether a worktree/session fixture is required or whether scoped contract coverage is sufficient for parity closure.

uncoveredSurfaceSemantics.partial: A partial surface must cite existing deterministic evidence, retain one or more coverage gaps, and still be missing at least one required planned-proof lane or planned deterministic fixture.
uncoveredSurfaceSemantics.uncovered: An uncovered surface has scoped contract metadata but no currently implemented required proof lane and must enumerate the missing deterministic fixture/report path needed for downstream work.
uncoveredSurfaceSemantics.covered: A covered surface is only truthful when every required lane is already implemented and no coverage gaps or planned deterministic fixtures remain.

proofClasses.name: report-contract
proofClasses.description: Machine-readable release/report surfaces that downstream gates can consume without rediscovering the underlying tests.

proofClasses.name: integration-contract
proofClasses.description: Deterministic integration tests that lock scoped behavior for a secondary surface.

proofClasses.name: unit-contract
proofClasses.description: Deterministic unit/schema contracts for lower-level secondary surfaces such as MCP.

proofClasses.name: repo-recording
proofClasses.description: Representative recorded repo/dev proof intended to show a realistic secondary-surface parity path without live reruns.

proofClasses.name: installed-recording
proofClasses.description: Representative recorded packaged/install proof intended to show the same parity surface under installed mode where required.

lanes.name: secondary-parity-report
lanes.title: Release-readable secondary parity report
lanes.description: Publishes the machine-readable report row that downstream release gates consume for web-mode parity.

lanes.name: integration:web-mode
lanes.title: Web-mode integration contract lane
lanes.description: Uses the tracked web-mode integration tests as the current deterministic proof lane for launch, onboarding, and session/recovery command-surface behavior.

lanes.name: repo-recording:web-mode
lanes.title: Representative repo-mode web recording
lanes.description: Defines a future deterministic repo/dev recording for browser-only web mode beyond the current integration contracts.

lanes.name: installed-recording:web-mode
lanes.title: Representative installed-mode web recording
lanes.description: Defines a future packaged web-mode parity recording if the matrix needs repo/install dual proof instead of a documented exception.

lanes.name: secondary-parity-report:mcp
lanes.title: Release-readable MCP parity report
lanes.description: Publishes the machine-readable report row that downstream release gates consume for MCP parity.

lanes.name: unit:mcp
lanes.title: MCP contract/unit lane
lanes.description: Uses the existing deterministic MCP server and client schema tests as the current scoped proof lane.

lanes.name: integration:mcp-session
lanes.title: Representative MCP interaction lane
lanes.description: Defines a future representative MCP client/server interaction recording for parity closure.

lanes.name: secondary-parity-report:workflow-bmad
lanes.title: Release-readable workflow parity report
lanes.description: Publishes the machine-readable report row that downstream release gates consume for representative workflow/BMAD parity.

lanes.name: integration:workflow-contracts
lanes.title: Workflow action contract lane
lanes.description: Uses tracked web workflow control/action contracts and command-routing coverage as the current deterministic workflow proof lane.

lanes.name: repo-recording:workflow-bmad
lanes.title: Representative workflow fixture lane
lanes.description: Defines a future deterministic workflow/BMAD fixture proving one representative end-to-end guided flow.

lanes.name: secondary-parity-report:worktree-session-recovery
lanes.title: Release-readable worktree/session parity report
lanes.description: Publishes the machine-readable report row that downstream release gates consume for worktree/session/recovery parity.

lanes.name: integration:session-recovery
lanes.title: Session and recovery integration lane
lanes.description: Uses tracked session, resume, headless, and web recovery diagnostics contracts as the current deterministic parity lane.

lanes.name: installed-recording:worktree-session-recovery
lanes.title: Representative installed worktree/session recording
lanes.description: Defines a future packaged representative worktree/session recovery recording if parity closure requires installed-mode proof beyond contract coverage.

reconciledFoundations.reportAnnotation: M113 cleanup requirements R023 and R026 are already validated closed foundation work, not an open parity gap for M114.
